title: Crispy Tofu with Smoky Black Beans & Spicy Orange Salsa
summary: I’ve heard that feijoada, the traditional Brazilian black bean stew, is sometimes eaten with slices of orange, to offset and freshen the richness in the beans. I got inspired by that flavour pairing to make this incredible dish. Whether you’re vegan, veggie or not, I promise you’ll love it.

method.description: Heat the oven to 200°C fan. Line a baking tray with baking parchment.

For the tofu, whisk the cornflour and paprika together in a bowl. Chop the tofu into 2cm cubes, then season with salt and toss through the cornflour mixture until evenly coated. Tip onto the prepared baking tray and drizzle with olive oil. Slide into the oven to bake for 20-30 mins until crispy and golden.

Meanwhile, for the beans, thinly slice the garlic and spring onions (separating the whites and greens). Place a saucepan over medium heat with a glug of olive oil. Once hot, add the spring onion whites and ½ the spring onion greens and stir-fry for 2-3 mins until wilted. Add the garlic and smoked paprika and cook for a minute until fragrant, then tip the black beans in, with their liquid and the bay leaf and season with salt and pepper. Bring to the boil, then turn the heat down. Leave to simmer until the tofu is crispy. Add a splash of water if it's getting dry. Before serving, taste and season with salt and pepper,if needed.

Whilst the beans are simmering, segment the oranges and mince the chili (as much as you would like to use). Toss the two together with the remaining spring onion greens. Squeeze any juices remaining from the orange remnants, then discard. Season with lime juice and salt.
To serve, divide the beans between bowls. Top with the orange salsa, crispy tofu and a drizzle of olive oil.

chefs.name: Chloe René
chefs.summary: Chloe (@chlo_eat) takes inspiration from her diverse upbringing to create recipes and dishes influenced from cultures around the world. Despite her classical training, Chloe's philosophy in the kitchen revolves around simplicity and home-cooking. She firmly believes that the essence of a great meal lies in the quality of ingredients and the care put into their preparation. Instead of relying on complex and technical processes, Chloe prizes the art of letting ingredients shine through, enhancing their natural flavours with a delicate touch.
